revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"
Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
Bot: wrap 3 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23368769069|time triggered pipeline]]

[[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Crossreference%2Fdoc here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Template shortcut|Xref|Crossref}} {{lua|Module:Hatnote inline}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|{{NAMESPACE}}:{{ROOTPAGENAME}}/styles.css}} The {{tlx|{{ROOTPAGENAME}}}} (a.k.a. {{tlx|crossref}} or {{tlx|xref}}) template formats text into a standardized style for an {{em|inline}} (not block-level) "''(See also ...)''"-type parenthetical [[:en:MOS:CROSSREF|Wikipedia crossreference]] to other Wikipedia material. When on different pages, these are [[WP:SELFREF|unprintworthy Wikipedia self-references]]. When crossreferencing material on the same page, they are not. For block-level crossreferences, see the {{tlx|Hatnote}} meta-template and its various progeny ({{tlx|See also}}, etc.). The output is ultimately produced by [[Module:Hatnote inline]]. It actually does support a block-level mode, however, for special cases (see parameter documentation below). See [[:Category:Cross-reference templates]] for other templates derived from or similar to this template. == Usage == The template does not automatically create links of any kind. Links and other desired formatting (round brackets, terminal punctuation) must be explicitly added, using normal [[Wikipedia:Cheatsheet|Wikipedia markup]]. '''Basic crossreference to another page:''' {{markup|{{Crossreference|(See also [[Radical Whigs]].)}}| {{ {{ROOTPAGENAME}}|(See also [[Radical Whigs]].)}}}} '''Basic crossreference on same page:''' {{markup|{{Crossreference|selfref=no|(see {{slink|#Parameters}})}}| {{ {{ROOTPAGENAME}}|selfref=no|(see {{slink|#Parameters}})}}}} '''All parameters:''' {{markup|{{Crossreference|class=class1 class2|selfref=no|inline=no|Blargh: * Foo * Bar * Baz }}| {{ {{ROOTPAGENAME}}|class=class1 class2|selfref=no|inline=no|Blargh: * Foo * Bar * Baz }} }} == Parameters == This template accepts the following parameters: * {{para|1}} or {{para|text}} or {{para|content}} – The cross-reference text (required). While the explicit naming of this parameter with {{para|1}}, {{para|text}}, or {{para|content}} is not required, it is always safest to use it this way, since any {{kbd|{{=}}}} character in the text will break the template if the parameter is unnamed. * {{para|class}} or {{para|extraclasses}} – Any extra CSS classes to be added. This should not normally be used. * {{para|selfref|no}} – If set to {{pval|no}} (or {{pval|n}}, {{pval|false}}, {{pval|0}}, etc.), removes the default CSS class selfref, used to denote unprintworthy self-references to Wikipedia {{crossreference|(see [[Template:Self-reference]] for more information)}}. This option should not be used on cross-article links, only links within the same article (and the shorthand templates {{tlx|See above}} and {{tlx|See below}} can be used for this purpose). The parameter aliases {{para|printworthy|yes}} or {{para|pw|yes}} (also with any other value, like {{pval|y}}, {{pval|true}}, {{pval|1}}, etc.) and {{para|unprintworthy|no}} (or {{pval|n}}, {{pval|false}}, {{pval|0}}) have the same effect as {{para|selfref|no}}. This can also be used for cross-references between internal Wikipedia pages such as guidelines and policies; there is no rationale to suppress the printing-out of links in this case. Note: These parameters are not yet "smart", and do not detect opposite values; that is, {{para|printworthy|{{!xt|no}}}} and {{para|unprintworthy|{{!xt|yes}}}} will not have the desired effect (and would be redundant anyway, since unprintworthiness is the default). * {{para|inline}} – Defaults to {{pval|yes}}. If set to {{pval|no}} (or {{pval|n}}, {{pval|false}}, {{pval|0}}, etc.), it will use a {{tag|div|o}} element instead of a {{tag|span|o}}, so you can use block-level elements inside it, such as a list. The difference between {{tnull|{{ROOTPAGENAME}}|inline{{=}}no|...}} and {{tlx|Hatnote|...}} is that the former is not indented the way the latter is. This de-indentation is done by [[WP:TemplateStyles]], specifically in [[Template:{{ROOTPAGENAME}}/styles.css]]. == CSS == This template always adds the CSS class crossreference, so you can use [[WP:User styles]] to do as you wish with it. You will need to use !important probably only in the case that you want to override this template's non-indentation when it is in block mode (i.e., when you do want it indented the way {{tlx|Hatnote}} works), and you'll be doing this with on-site user style, such as [[Special:MyPage/common.css]]. This is because TemplateStyles actually load after on-site user styles, so you must override the TemplateStyle with the !important priority flag. == Redirects == The following alternative names redirect to this template: * {{tlx|xref}} * {{tlx|crossref}} * {{tlx|cross-ref}} * {{tlx|cross-reference}} == See also == * {{tlx|Hatnote inline}} – the meta-template this template uses, and other templates that use it * {{tlx|See above}} and {{tlx|See below}} – some specific, printworthy "child" templates of {{tlx|{{ROOTPAGENAME}}}} * [[:en:Template:Hatnote templates documentation|Template:Hatnote templates documentation]] – docs for all the block-format hatnotes == Template data == {{TemplateData header}} { "params": { "text": { "aliases": [ "1", "content" ] }, "class": { "aliases": [ "extraclasses" ], "label": "CSS classes" }, "inline": { "type": "boolean" }, "printworthy": { "aliases": [ "selfref" ], "type": "boolean" } }, "description": "formats text into a standardized style for an inline (not block-level) \"(See also ...)\"-type parenthetical Wikipedia crossreference to other Wikipedia material.", "paramOrder": [ "text", "printworthy", "class", "inline" ], "format": "inline" } {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Cross-reference templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Inline hatnote templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24926181992|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Template shortcut|Xref|Crossref}} {{lua|Module:Hatnote inline}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|{{NAMESPACE}}:{{ROOTPAGENAME}}/styles.css}} The {{tlx|{{ROOTPAGENAME}}}} (a.k.a. {{tlx|crossref}} or {{tlx|xref}}) template formats text into a standardized style for an {{em|inline}} (not block-level) "''(See also ...)''"-type parenthetical [[:en:MOS:CROSSREF|Wikipedia crossreference]] to other Wikipedia material. When on different pages, these are [[WP:SELFREF|unprintworthy Wikipedia self-references]]. When crossreferencing material on the same page, they are not. For block-level crossreferences, see the {{tlx|Hatnote}} meta-template and its various progeny ({{tlx|See also}}, etc.). The output is ultimately produced by [[Module:Hatnote inline]]. It actually does support a block-level mode, however, for special cases (see parameter documentation below). See [[:Category:Cross-reference templates]] for other templates derived from or similar to this template. == Usage == The template does not automatically create links of any kind. Links and other desired formatting (round brackets, terminal punctuation) must be explicitly added, using normal [[Wikipedia:Cheatsheet|Wikipedia markup]]. '''Basic crossreference to another page:''' {{markup|{{Crossreference|(See also [[Radical Whigs]].)}}| {{ {{ROOTPAGENAME}}|(See also [[Radical Whigs]].)}}}} '''Basic crossreference on same page:''' {{markup|{{Crossreference|selfref=no|(see {{slink|#Parameters}})}}| {{ {{ROOTPAGENAME}}|selfref=no|(see {{slink|#Parameters}})}}}} '''All parameters:''' {{markup|{{Crossreference|class=class1 class2|selfref=no|inline=no|Blargh: * Foo * Bar * Baz }}| {{ {{ROOTPAGENAME}}|class=class1 class2|selfref=no|inline=no|Blargh: * Foo * Bar * Baz }} }} == Parameters == This template accepts the following parameters: * {{para|1}} or {{para|text}} or {{para|content}} – The cross-reference text (required). While the explicit naming of this parameter with {{para|1}}, {{para|text}}, or {{para|content}} is not required, it is always safest to use it this way, since any {{kbd|{{=}}}} character in the text will break the template if the parameter is unnamed. * {{para|class}} or {{para|extraclasses}} – Any extra CSS classes to be added. This should not normally be used. * {{para|selfref|no}} – If set to {{pval|no}} (or {{pval|n}}, {{pval|false}}, {{pval|0}}, etc.), removes the default CSS class selfref, used to denote unprintworthy self-references to Wikipedia {{crossreference|(see [[Template:Self-reference]] for more information)}}. This option should not be used on cross-article links, only links within the same article (and the shorthand templates {{tlx|See above}} and {{tlx|See below}} can be used for this purpose). The parameter aliases {{para|printworthy|yes}} or {{para|pw|yes}} (also with any other value, like {{pval|y}}, {{pval|true}}, {{pval|1}}, etc.) and {{para|unprintworthy|no}} (or {{pval|n}}, {{pval|false}}, {{pval|0}}) have the same effect as {{para|selfref|no}}. This can also be used for cross-references between internal Wikipedia pages such as guidelines and policies; there is no rationale to suppress the printing-out of links in this case. Note: These parameters are not yet "smart", and do not detect opposite values; that is, {{para|printworthy|{{!xt|no}}}} and {{para|unprintworthy|{{!xt|yes}}}} will not have the desired effect (and would be redundant anyway, since unprintworthiness is the default). * {{para|inline}} – Defaults to {{pval|yes}}. If set to {{pval|no}} (or {{pval|n}}, {{pval|false}}, {{pval|0}}, etc.), it will use a {{tag|div|o}} element instead of a {{tag|span|o}}, so you can use block-level elements inside it, such as a list. The difference between {{tnull|{{ROOTPAGENAME}}|inline{{=}}no|...}} and {{tlx|Hatnote|...}} is that the former is not indented the way the latter is. This de-indentation is done by [[WP:TemplateStyles]], specifically in [[Template:{{ROOTPAGENAME}}/styles.css]]. == CSS == This template always adds the CSS class crossreference, so you can use [[WP:User styles]] to do as you wish with it. You will need to use !important probably only in the case that you want to override this template's non-indentation when it is in block mode (i.e., when you do want it indented the way {{tlx|Hatnote}} works), and you'll be doing this with on-site user style, such as [[Special:MyPage/common.css]]. This is because TemplateStyles actually load after on-site user styles, so you must override the TemplateStyle with the !important priority flag. == Redirects == The following alternative names redirect to this template: * {{tlx|xref}} * {{tlx|crossref}} * {{tlx|cross-ref}} * {{tlx|cross-reference}} == See also == * {{tlx|Hatnote inline}} – the meta-template this template uses, and other templates that use it * {{tlx|See above}} and {{tlx|See below}} – some specific, printworthy "child" templates of {{tlx|{{ROOTPAGENAME}}}} * [[:en:Template:Hatnote templates documentation|Template:Hatnote templates documentation]] – docs for all the block-format hatnotes == Template data == {{TemplateData header}} { "params": { "text": { "aliases": [ "1", "content" ] }, "class": { "aliases": [ "extraclasses" ], "label": "CSS classes" }, "inline": { "type": "boolean" }, "printworthy": { "aliases": [ "selfref" ], "type": "boolean" } }, "description": "formats text into a standardized style for an inline (not block-level) \"(See also ...)\"-type parenthetical Wikipedia crossreference to other Wikipedia material.", "paramOrder": [ "text", "printworthy", "class", "inline" ], "format": "inline" } {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Cross-reference templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Inline hatnote templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
